NEXI Nature Expression of Intelligence
GitHub
A field guide to intelligence beyond the neuron

Nature already
solved intelligence
many ways.

NEXI mines how intelligence is expressed across biological species and turns it into falsifiable, engineerable architecture patterns for AI agents.

Pillar 01 — World models

LLMs predict text,
not consequences.

Scaling token prediction does not build an internal model of the world. Feed agents principles & metamodels that let them compose world-context and reason about actions.

Pillar 02 — Beyond the perceptron

All of AI descends
from one neuron.

Deep learning traces to a single template — the mammalian cortical neuron. Intelligence takes many forms; the perceptron lineage has explored a sliver of the design space.

A research program by Juan Gerardo Castro Sanchez · grounded in three curated knowledge vaults & one open pattern catalog
01The wager

Two gaps in modern AI. One opportunity between them.

The agentic era is a chance to combine today's LLMs with architectural primitives borrowed from organisms that compute nothing like us — fungi, octopuses, birds, bacteria, viruses.

Pillar 01 · Guiding principle

The world-model gap

Yann LeCun's critique: a system that only predicts the next token cannot plan, cannot model cause and effect, cannot ground language in a world. The fix isn't more scale — it's structure that represents consequences.

Guiding principle for what we give the machine.
Pillar 02 · The search space

The biological fan

Intelligence evolved independently across phyla — acerebrate optimization in slime molds, distributed cognition in bird flocks, quorum decisions in bacteria, embodiment without cortex in octopuses. Each is an unborrowed design pattern.

The opportunity space we search for the machine.
02The system

Four assets, one assembly line from biology to architecture.

Discovery is grounded in peer-reviewed science, engineered into falsifiable patterns, validated against real AI gaps, and held to publication-grade rigor.

nature evidence flows right AI gaps pull the search rigor underwrites it all
03Worked example · end to end

A zebra finch eavesdrops. An AI agent gets a world model.

One thread, all four assets — how a bird watching a fight becomes a drop-in pattern for multi-agent systems.

Source · peer-reviewedBiology

Wild zebra finches extract information from fights they only watch

Communication Networks of Wild Taeniopygia castanotis — bioRxiv, 2026. Finches that observe third-party song interactions learn who is dominant, who is bonded, who is a threat — without ever participating.

Vault · discoveryNature Intelligence

Distilled into a principle, a hub, and a metamodel

The observation is curated into an atomic principle, consolidated with corroborating sources, and abstracted into a reusable architectural metamodel.

paper → P02 Eavesdropping as Network Mechanism → hub Eavesdropping → MM1 Distributed Information Substrate
Repo · engineeringNEXI pattern

Turned into a falsifiable, engineerable pattern

The hub graduates into the catalog as eavesdropping — a machine-readable pattern with a testable claim and drop-in formats (architecture + skill). 

# nexi/eavesdropping/nexi.yaml
abstract_claim: Agents that observe but do not participate can still
  extract goals, knowledge, capabilities & relationships.
falsifiable_hypothesis: Multi-agent RL with attention over
  observed third-party interactions reaches threshold in
  ≥10% fewer episodes than self-observation-only baselines.
applies_to: [multi-agent, world-models, llm-orchestration, dec-POMDP]
formats: [architecture, skill]
provenance: {hub: Eavesdropping, principle: P02, metamodel: MM1}
Vault · demandAI Research gap

Aimed at a documented capability gap

The pattern targets a real limitation in the SOTA catalog: agents in partially-observable, multi-agent settings that cannot build peer models from indirect observation. The gap's nature_evidence field now has a candidate.

Vault · rigorScientific Writing

Held to publication-grade standards

Provenance, falsifiability, and honest tensions make the pattern defensible — ready for the white paper and, ultimately, a peer-reviewed claim rather than a metaphor.

04Your lens · switch above
For AI Engineers

Patterns you can drop into a harness — each with a hypothesis you can break.

User

You build agentic and multi-agent systems. You want architectural leverage, not another bio-metaphor.

Context

You're deciding whether a pattern earns a place in your context / memory / harness stack. Your first question is "is this real, and does it measure up?"

Content

Every NEXI ships a falsifiable hypothesis, an applies_to scope, an architecture spec, and a framework-neutral skill file — with provenance back to the biology.

Machine-readable schema

nexi.yaml validated against a JSON schema — patterns as data, not prose.

Falsifiable, not vibes

Each claim is a hypothesis with a threshold you can run and refute.

Drop-in skill files

Framework-neutral skill.md specs for context, memory & harness engineering.

Composable clusters

Patterns combine into intelligence models — co-dependent systems, the real unit of adoption.

For Scientific Researchers

A traceable chain from peer-reviewed finding to engineering claim.

User

You work in comparative cognition, biology, or AI. You may collaborate, review, or co-author.

Context

You care about provenance, evidentiary standards, and whether a claim is novel and defensible enough to publish.

Content

A documented curation gate, full citation trails, and an honest tensions log — plus a structured white paper the findings feed into.

Promotion gate

A hub graduates only with ≥2 principles across ≥2 independent sources.

Full provenance trail

paper → P## → hub → MM# → pattern, every link cited.

Tensions, not consensus

Cross-source conflicts are logged as open questions — the research signal.

Publication-ready

A 10-section white paper with references, built to survive peer review.

06The business lens
Why a program like this creates value

A defensible catalog of AI design patterns, mined from 3.8 billion years of R&D.

01 · The bet

An untapped design space

Everyone is scaling the same architecture. The frontier advantage goes to whoever borrows primitives no one else has — biology is a vast, unexplored library of proven intelligence.

02 · The moat

Provenance & falsifiability

Not inspiration decks — a curated, cited, testable pattern catalog with a documented method. That rigor is the difference between defensible IP and a metaphor.

03 · Where value lands

Better agents, real credibility

Sample-efficient, world-grounded agents for partial-observability problems; a reusable pattern library for teams; and peer-reviewed publications that compound trust.

07How the catalog earns trust

Baseline → Benchmark → Benchbreak

A pattern's authority is earned in three stages — from AI-proposed, to evidenced by independent science, to proven in the hands of builders. It is also the transparency model: the AI proposes, the evidence validates, the community decides.

STAGE 01 · PROPOSED

Baseline

What AI can produce. Under human direction, Claude processes the literature, runs deep analysis and meta-modeling, extracts principles, and proposes candidate NEXIs. Fast and generative — but on its own a well-formed hypothesis, not evidence.

authority: proposed
STAGE 02 · EVIDENCED

Benchmark

Corroboration by independent science. A NEXI graduates when principles from several separate publications converge on the same mechanism. Cross-source agreement is the validation signal — not one study, not one model's reasoning.

authority: evidenced
STAGE 03 · PROVEN IN USE

Benchbreak

Validation by the community. An engineer adopts a NEXI as a baseline, tests it on a real problem, and returns feedback — or improves the pattern and contributes it back. The catalog doing real work in the world, and being refined by it.

authority: proven in use

Early adopters — this is the invitation: use a NEXI, put it under load, break it, and tell us what happened.

VALIDATED SO FAR

Seven patterns have completed the dual-standpoint review — each assessed against documented AI capability gaps and for faithfulness to its sources.

  • eavesdropping AI-research: Grounded Scientific-writing: Aligned

    Monitor third-party interactions to build peer models cheaply — addresses machine theory of mind and partial-observability inference.

  • identity-by-pattern AI-research: Grounded Scientific-writing: Aligned

    Recover individual identity from observation using embeddings trained for within-class discriminability — addresses partner re-identification under partial observability and behaviour-based opponent recognition.

  • context-bound-semantics AI-research: Relevant Scientific-writing: Aligned

    Bind a signal's meaning to its context — an enabler for shared-meaning bootstrapping and peer-state modelling. Grounded at enabler level, honestly recorded as Relevant.

  • negative-evidence-reasoning AI-research: Grounded Scientific-writing: Aligned

    Read information from an expected-but-missing signal — addresses the documented language-model failure to reason about negation and absence.

  • social-hotspots AI-research: Relevant Scientific-writing: Aligned

    Concentrate observation and memory where agents converge — an enabler for coordinated multi-agent exploration, positioned against prioritized experience replay.

  • multi-modal-integration AI-research: Relevant Scientific-writing: Aligned

    Encode multiple channels into one shared latent space — a single-source draft grounded in cross-phyletic neuroanatomy. Relevant rather than Grounded: it largely re-describes established joint-encoding practice (CLIP / ImageBind).

  • cognitive-regime-selection AI-research: Relevant Scientific-writing: Aligned

    Match a deployment niche to one of three regimes on a continuous optimality surface. Its novel part is a design-time selection criterion; much of the rest is established deployment-tier practice.

08Engage

Come in through your door.

If you build agents

Adopt & break a pattern

  • Read a pattern's architecture + skill spec
  • Run its falsifiable hypothesis against your baseline
  • Compose patterns into a cluster for your stack
Browse the catalog →
If you do science

Scrutinize & collaborate

  • Trace the provenance chain from paper to pattern
  • Interrogate the curation method & tensions log
  • Co-author the white paper or a new NEXI
Read the white paper →
